name: Travel Personality
description: Where in the world should you go next? Find out your ideal travel style and destination!

adventurer.title: The Adventurer
adventurer.description: You crave adrenaline, wide open spaces and experiences that push your limits. Trekking through rainforests, bungee jumping or navigating unmarked trails — you're all in. The beaten path is just a starting point for you.
characteristics: Drawn to remote and wild places
Stories to tell for a lifetime
adventurer.strengths: Your fearlessness and adaptability mean you get the most authentic, transformative travel experiences. You go where others won't.
adventurer.challenges: Planning and logistics can frustrate you. Learning to balance spontaneity with a basic safety net will make your adventures even richer.

culturalist.title: The Culture Seeker
culturalist.description: Museums, ancient ruins, local festivals and traditional cuisine are your idea of paradise. You travel to understand people, history and the richness of human civilisation. Every trip is a masterclass in the world.
culturalist.strengths: You return home genuinely transformed, with deep knowledge and meaningful connections that casual tourists never find.
culturalist.challenges: You can get overwhelmed trying to see everything. Prioritise a few experiences and allow yourself to sit still and soak it in.

relaxer.title: The Sun & Soul Relaxer
relaxer.description: Beach, pool, cocktail in hand. You travel to recharge, disconnect and savour pure relaxation. Lazy mornings, long lunches and stunning sunsets are your perfect itinerary. You've earned it.
characteristics: Drawn to warm climates and beautiful scenery
relaxer.strengths: You know that rest is productive. Your holidays actually restore your energy and you come back ready to conquer the world.
relaxer.challenges: You might miss some incredible local experiences by staying in the resort. Even one local excursion can elevate a relaxing trip.

socialiser.title: The Social Explorer
socialiser.description: You travel for the people. Whether it's hostel common rooms, guided group tours or solo trips where you somehow make 20 friends by day two — connection is your currency. You leave with friends all over the world.
characteristics: Loves group activities and shared experiences
socialiser.strengths: Your openness and warmth mean locals and fellow travellers open doors for you that no guidebook can find.
socialiser.challenges: Make sure to carve out moments for solo reflection. Some of travel's best insights arrive in quiet.

questions.question: When you're booking a holiday, what excites you most?
options: Finding a wild hike or extreme activity
Choosing museums, galleries and local history to explore
Picking the resort with the best pool and beach
Seeing which friends might join or who I'll meet

questions.question: Your ideal accommodation is:
options: A tent under the stars or a remote mountain hut
A boutique guesthouse in a historic city centre
An all-inclusive resort with sea views
A lively hostel with a social bar and shared spaces

questions.question: What does a perfect travel day look like?
options: White-water rafting in the morning, jungle trek in the afternoon
Morning at a local market, afternoon in a museum, evening at a heritage restaurant
Sleep in, beach until sunset, dinner at a beachside restaurant
Morning coffee with new friends, group day trip, dinner party back at the hostel

questions.question: Which destination most appeals to you?
options: Patagonia, Himalayas or Iceland
Rome, Kyoto or Istanbul
Maldives, Bali or the Amalfi Coast
Barcelona, Bangkok or Buenos Aires

questions.question: When you arrive somewhere new, you immediately:
options: Ask a local about the nearest trail, cliff dive or hidden adventure
Head to the old town and start exploring the streets
Check into the hotel, order room service and decompress
Download a meetup app and see who's around tonight

questions.question: Your travel souvenir of choice is:
options: A scar, a patch on your backpack or a wild story
A handmade craft, a book or a piece of local art
A new swimsuit you bought by the pool
A friend's contact saved in your phone

questions.question: On a long-haul flight, you are most likely found:
options: Planning your next spontaneous detour on Google Maps
Reading a history or travel book about your destination
Sleeping with a neck pillow, fully reclined
Chatting to the person next to you and swapping travel tips

questions.question: What would ruin a holiday for you most?
options: Being stuck in a resort with nothing adventurous to do
Having no time to visit cultural sites or local landmarks
Bad weather stopping you relaxing on the beach
Travelling alone with nobody to share experiences with
